steps.action: Run `bun cli/src/cli.ts skills -n` in TTY session (HOME=/tmp/af-utv-cli-core-u3)

steps.expected: Focus moves from User to Project
steps.observed: Selection indicator changed from `● User` to `● Project`.

steps.expected: Project value is confirmed and flow advances
steps.observed: `Install to:` rendered as `Project (./.claude/)` and next `Agents/targets:` prompt opened.

terminalSnapshots: Before nav: `◆  Install to:` with `● User` / `○ Project`
After down: `◆  Install to:` with `○ User` / `● Project`
After enter: `◇  Install to: Project (./.claude/)` then `◆  Agents/targets:`
evidence.exitCodes: Navigation/confirmation flow continued normally; command later cancelled intentionally in another assertion stage.

steps.action: Run `bun cli/src/cli.ts install -n` and reach `What do you want to install?`
Press `space` on Skills, move down twice, press `space` on Subagents
steps.expected: Only Skills and Subagents are toggled on

steps.observed: Next screen summarized `What do you want to install? Skills, Subagents` before `Install to:`.

terminalSnapshots: Confirmation summary: `◇  What do you want to install?` then `Skills, Subagents`
evidence.exitCodes: Flow advanced correctly after Enter; later cancelled intentionally for cancellation checks.

assertions.title: Prompt cancellation is graceful and consistent

steps.action: Cancel at category stage (`What do you want to install?`) via Ctrl+C
steps.expected: Graceful cancellation, no stack trace, consistent exit behavior
steps.observed: Rendered `Operation cancelled.` and returned to shell; `CATEGORY_CANCEL_EXIT:0`.

steps.action: Cancel at target stage (`Agents/targets:`) via Ctrl+C
steps.observed: Rendered `Operation cancelled.` and returned to shell; `STAGE_TARGET_CANCEL_EXIT:0` and `RUN2_CANCEL_EXIT:0`.

steps.action: Cancel at item stage (`Skills (1/1)`) via Ctrl+C
steps.observed: Rendered `Operation cancelled.` and returned to shell; `ITEM_CANCEL_EXIT:0`.

terminalSnapshots: Category cancel snapshot includes `■  What do you want to install?` then `Operation cancelled.`
Target cancel snapshot includes `■  Agents/targets:` then `Operation cancelled.`
Item cancel snapshot includes `■  Skills (1/1)` then `Operation cancelled.`

steps.observed: `git status --porcelain` baseline: `M .factory/library/user-testing.md`; install target paths absent in repo and assigned HOME.

steps.expected: No writes to install targets or repo from cancellation
steps.observed: Post-checks showed `.claude`, `.codex`, `.factorydroid`, `.rulesync`, `rulesync.jsonc` all absent in repo and HOME namespace.
No installation artifacts created; git status noise included unrelated parallel validation output path outside this flow's actions.

assertions.title: No keypress bleed between prompt steps

steps.action: Confirm category multi-select with Enter in `install -n` flow
steps.expected: Next step appears and waits; Enter does not auto-confirm next prompt
steps.observed: After Enter, `Install to:` appeared and stayed interactive (no auto-advance).

steps.expected: Flow moves only one stage to `Agents/targets:` and waits for new input
steps.observed: `Agents/targets:` appeared with all options unchecked and remained waiting after idle check.

terminalSnapshots: Post-level-enter: `◆  Agents/targets:` prompt active with `◻ claudecode/codexcli/factorydroid`
After wait-idle: still on same `Agents/targets:` prompt (not skipped)

frictions.description: Repository `git status` changed during run due parallel validator outputs under `.factory/validation/cli-core/user-testing/`, so side-effect confirmation relied on install-target path checks in repo + assigned HOME namespace.
frictions.resolution: Validated cancellation side effects via explicit filesystem checks for target paths (`.claude`, `.codex`, `.factorydroid`, `.rulesync`, `rulesync.jsonc`) before/after cancellations.

summary: Tested 5 prompt UX assertions (VAL-PROMPTS-001..005): all 5 passed. Cancellation was graceful/consistent at category, target, and item stages with exit code 0 and no stack traces; no install-target side effects were observed in repo or assigned HOME namespace.
